Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Reference DOM (Question number prefixes): Fields prefixed with Q7. Q12. style question numbers Reference DOM (Ashby yes no buttons): Ashby-inspired yes/no as two styled buttons instead of native radio Reference DOM (Title attribute tooltip label): Field identified primarily via title attribute on input with minimal label Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: shadow-host, aria-labelledby-hidden, label-wraps-deep, decoy-form, pill-yes-no. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Question number prefixes; Ashby yes no buttons; Title attribute tooltip label.
